Alibaba Denies Report It Helped China Identify Military Targets in U.S. -- Update
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan


Alibaba Group denied a report that said it provides tech support for the Chinese military to target the U.S.

"The assertions and innuendos in the article are completely false," Alibaba said in a statement.

The Financial Times reported Friday that it viewed a White House national security memo that includes declassified intelligence on how Alibaba supplies the People's Liberation Army with capabilities that could threaten U.S. security.

The Financial Times said in its report that it couldn't independently verify the claims.

Alibaba provides the Chinese government and PLA with access to customer data including IP addresses, WiFi information and payment records, the Financial Times reported, citing the memo.

"We question the motivation behind the anonymous leak," Alibaba said in its statement.

The Financial Times said in a statement, "We stand by our reporting."

American depositary shares of Alibaba closed down 3.8% at $153.80 on Friday.


Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-14-25 1652ET

September Jobs Report Will Come Out on Nov. 20
data.description:
The Bureau of Labor Statistics will release the September jobs report Thursday, after it was delayed due to the government shutdown. Investors are hoping the new data can shed light on the state of the US economy. Gene Sperling, former director of the National Economic Council, had to work through a government shutdown in 2013. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close."

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

Berkshire buys shares of Google parent Alphabet, sells more Apple
data.description:
(Reuters) -Berkshire Hathaway on Friday revealed a $4.3 billion stake in Google parent Alphabet and said it further reduced its stake in Apple, detailing its equity portfolio for the last time before Warren Buffett ends his 60-year run as chief executive.

In a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, Berkshire said it owned 17.85 million Alphabet shares as of September 30.

Berkshire lowered its Apple stake to 238.2 million shares from 280 million in the third quarter, and has now sold nearly three-quarters of the 905 million shares it once held.

The filing listed Berkshire's U.S.-listed stock holdings as of September 30, which comprised most of the conglomerate's $283.2 billion equity portfolio.

(Reporting by Jonathan Stempel in New YorkEditing by Matthew Lewis)

Warner Bros. Discovery Prepares for Possible Sale
data.description:
Paramount (PHRE), Netflix (NFLX) and Comcast (CMCSA) are eyeing potential bids for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). The company, meanwhile, has amended CEO David Zaslav's contract to secure his pay in the event of a sale. Bloomberg's Felix Gillette speaks with Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech."
